questions.question: You benchmark a model and report 'average 12ms per image on a 4090'. What is missing?
options: Nothing; that is a complete benchmark
Percentiles (p50/p95/p99), warmup discipline, and CUDA synchronisation; a mean alone hides tail latency and may measure kernel dispatch instead of kernel execution
questions.explanation: Production latency is measured in percentiles because real-time systems fail on tails, not averages. Without warmup, JIT compilation inflates the first few measurements. Without torch.cuda.synchronize, GPU kernel launches return before the kernel finishes, so you measure dispatch, not execution. All three have to be right for the number to be meaningful.
questions.question_zh: 你在 4090 上 benchmark 一个模型，报告 “平均每张图 12ms”。这里少了什么？
options_zh: 没少什么，这就是完整的 benchmark
百分位数（p50/p95/p99）、warmup 纪律、CUDA 同步；只看均值会掩盖尾部延迟，并且可能测的是 kernel dispatch 而不是 kernel 真正的执行时间
questions.explanation_zh: 生产环境的延迟必须看百分位数，因为实时系统死于尾部而不是平均值。没做 warmup 时，JIT 编译会让最初几次测量虚高。没调用 torch.cuda.synchronize 时，GPU kernel 启动后立即返回，你测到的是 dispatch 而不是 execution。三者都做对，数字才有意义。

questions.question: FLOPs and on-device latency do not correlate perfectly. Why?
options: Different operations have different hardware friendliness; depthwise convolutions may have lower FLOPs but are memory-bound on GPUs, while dense convolutions use Tensor Cores efficiently. Memory bandwidth, kernel launch overhead, and cache behaviour also shape latency beyond raw FLOPs
questions.explanation: FLOPs is a cheap proxy. Wall-clock latency depends on how well an operation matches the hardware's strengths: dense matmuls pin Tensor Cores; depthwise convs stall on memory bandwidth; attention stalls on sequence-length^2 memory reads. For architecture search FLOPs is useful; for deployment decisions measure on the target device.
questions.question_zh: FLOPs 和实际设备上的延迟并不完全相关，为什么？
options_zh: 不同算子对硬件的友好程度不同；depthwise 卷积 FLOPs 更低，但在 GPU 上是显存带宽 bound，而 dense 卷积能高效用上 Tensor Core。除了 FLOPs，显存带宽、kernel 启动开销、cache 行为也影响延迟
PyTorch 的 FLOPs 计数有问题
questions.explanation_zh: FLOPs 只是一个廉价代理。真正的 wall-clock 延迟取决于算子和硬件强项的匹配程度：dense matmul 把 Tensor Core 跑满；depthwise 卷积卡在显存带宽；attention 卡在序列长度平方级别的显存读取。架构搜索时 FLOPs 有用；但部署决策必须在目标设备上实测。

questions.question: Post-training static INT8 quantisation typically loses how much accuracy on ImageNet-class vision models?
options: 0.1-1 percentage points when properly calibrated; batch-norm-fused conv models are particularly well-behaved under INT8
questions.explanation: Static PTQ on well-calibrated vision models loses a fraction of a point to about 1 point. The bigger loss modes are (a) insufficient calibration data, (b) quantising activations with extreme outliers (fix with per-channel quantisation or clipping), (c) un-fused BatchNorm layers. When those are handled, INT8 is essentially free.
questions.question_zh: ImageNet 类视觉模型在做训练后静态 INT8 量化时，通常会损失多少精度？
options_zh: 校准得当时大约 0.1-1 个百分点；融合了 BatchNorm 的卷积模型对 INT8 特别友好
questions.explanation_zh: 校准良好的视觉模型做 PTQ，损失从零点几到大约 1 个百分点。损失变大的常见原因是：(a) 校准数据不足；(b) 给带极端 outlier 的激活做量化（用 per-channel 量化或 clip 解决）；(c) 没融合的 BatchNorm 层。把这些处理好之后，INT8 基本免费。

questions.question: Your mobile app needs a vision model under 10MB with sub-10ms latency. Which backbone do you pick?
options: MobileNetV3-Small or EfficientNet-Lite-B0 quantised to INT8; both target this budget and compile cleanly to TFLite / Core ML
questions.explanation: Mobile vision shipping in 2026 is still dominated by MobileNetV3 and EfficientNet-Lite variants because they were designed for this budget: depthwise convs, h-swish, squeeze-excite, quantisation-aware from day one. ResNet-50 and ViT-Base are 100MB+ in FP32 and 25MB+ in INT8, blowing the size budget before latency is even measured.
questions.question_zh: 你的移动端 app 需要一个 10MB 以下、延迟低于 10ms 的视觉模型，应该选哪个 backbone？
options_zh: MobileNetV3-Small 或 EfficientNet-Lite-B0，量化到 INT8；它们就是为这个预算设计的，能干净地导出到 TFLite/Core ML
questions.explanation_zh: 2026 年的移动端视觉部署仍然由 MobileNetV3 和 EfficientNet-Lite 主导，因为它们从设计之初就瞄准这个预算：depthwise 卷积、h-swish、squeeze-excite，并且天然支持量化。ResNet-50 和 ViT-Base 在 FP32 下超过 100MB，INT8 也有 25MB+，连尺寸预算都没法满足，更别提延迟。

questions.question: You export a PyTorch model to ONNX with opset 17 and it fails with 'Unsupported operator'. What are the most likely causes?
options: (1) A custom op that has no ONNX mapping; (2) a non-deterministic control-flow path that tracing cannot capture; (3) a tensor-scalar interaction that is implicit in Python but has no ONNX equivalent. Fixes: replace the op, use `torch.jit.script` for control flow, or upgrade opset
The model has too many parameters
questions.explanation: ONNX export is tracing by default, so anything that tracing cannot capture — runtime control flow, Python-level branches, custom CUDA ops — fails. Most failures are a single node that has no ONNX equivalent (often a tiny custom op or a call to torch.special.*). The fix is either replacing the op, switching to `torch.jit.script`, or moving to a newer opset where the op exists.
questions.question_zh: 你把 PyTorch 模型导出为 opset 17 的 ONNX，结果报 “Unsupported operator”。最可能的原因是什么？
options_zh: (1) 自定义算子没有 ONNX 映射；(2) tracing 抓不到的非确定性控制流；(3) Python 里隐式的 tensor-标量交互在 ONNX 里没有等价物。修复方式：替换算子、用 `torch.jit.script` 处理控制流，或升级 opset
questions.explanation_zh: ONNX 默认通过 tracing 导出，所以 tracing 抓不到的东西都会失败，比如运行时控制流、Python 级别的分支、自定义 CUDA 算子。大多数失败都是某一个节点没有 ONNX 等价（往往是某个小自定义算子或 torch.special.* 调用）。修复方式是替换算子、改用 `torch.jit.script`，或迁移到支持该算子的更新 opset。
